title: EU Watchdog EBA Details Big Crypto Fines as Landmark Laws Bite
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) has detailed a penalty framework that can strip non-compliant significant token issuers of up to 12.5% of their annual revenue. This move comes as the EU's landmark crypto regulations take effect, aiming to enforce stricter oversight in the industry.
body:
The European Banking Authority (EBA) has detailed a significant penalty framework for non-compliant crypto firms, specifically targeting significant token issuers, who could face fines of up to 12.5% of their annual revenue. This announcement comes as the EU's landmark crypto regulations, known as the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), begin to be enforced across the region.

The proposed fines are designed to ensure that significant token issuers and crypto service providers adhere to the new regulatory standards. The EBA's framework includes penalties for various compliance failures, such as inadequate consumer protection measures, poor risk management, and non-compliance with anti-money laundering (AML) rules. The EBA aims to create a more transparent and secure crypto market within the EU.

This development is crucial for everyday crypto users as it signals a shift towards greater regulatory oversight in the industry. Stricter rules and potential fines could lead to more secure and reliable crypto services, reducing the risk of fraud and market manipulation. However, it may also increase operational costs for crypto firms, which could potentially be passed on to consumers.

Crypto users and investors should watch for further details on how these penalties will be implemented and enforced. The EBA's framework is part of a broader effort to integrate crypto assets into the traditional financial system while ensuring consumer protection. For now, staying informed about regulatory changes and choosing compliant crypto services will be key for users navigating this evolving landscape.
twitterThread: The EU is getting serious about crypto regulation. Non-compliant significant token issuers could face fines of up to 12.5% of their annual revenue. 🚨

The European Banking Authority (EBA) has detailed a new penalty framework to enforce the EU's landmark crypto laws. This includes hefty fines for failing to meet consumer protection and AML standards. 📜

If you're using crypto services in the EU, keep an eye on how these new rules affect your favorite platforms. Compliance is key—read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-28-eu-proposes-huge-fines-for-non-compliant-crypto-firms
